title: Tokenized Fund Management: Running Raises, Vesting and Distributions
content_html:
Token-based funds and investment pools have operational needs that equity-era tooling never anticipated. Positions live in wallets across many chains. Investor stakes are denominated in tokens with vesting schedules. Distributions are on-chain transfers, not wire runs. Managing this well is what tokenized fund management means in practice.

The three jobs of a token fund's back office

First, know the holdings. A token fund typically holds assets across multiple chains and dozens of wallets, and the books are only as good as the completeness of that view. Second, manage the obligations. Every investor stake carries terms: an allocation, a vesting schedule, and a distribution path. Third, prove both. Investors and auditors expect the fund to show holdings and obligations from records, not from memory.

Where Cryptool fits

Cryptool was built around exactly these three jobs.

  • Holdings: the Portfolio module aggregates every wallet across all chains and wallet providers into one non-custodial, read-only view.
  • Obligations: the Raise module creates pools with defined vesting schedules, records each member's allocation, and runs distributions according to the schedule.
  • Proof: because holdings and obligations live in one account, reporting starts from live records rather than reconciled spreadsheets.

On-chain modules run on EVM chains and MultiversX, with more coming.

Common questions

What is tokenized fund management?
The operational management of a fund whose positions and investor stakes are token-based: tracking multi-chain holdings, applying vesting schedules, and running on-chain distributions.

Does this require giving up custody?
No. Cryptool's portfolio tracking is read only and non-custodial. Keys stay with the fund.

Which chains are supported?
Portfolio tracking covers all chains and wallet providers. On-chain modules for raises and distributions run on EVM chains and MultiversX, with more coming.

What does it cost?
Current plans are on the services and pricing page.
